title: European Heritage Hub announces two European Heritage Label sites to host upcoming Intergenerational Climate Conversations
content: The EU-funded European Heritage Hub has selected two European Heritage Label sites to host the first-ever Intergenerational Climate Conversations, an initiative co-coordinated by Hub partners ICLEI Europe and Europa Nostra, with support from the European Heritage Label Bureau and their Youth Advisory Board, and Youth.Heritage.Europe. alongside the

creator: Liv Martin

title: Call for Applications: Hub Peer-learning Visit to Rotterdam
content:
The EU-funded European Heritage Hub invites city representatives and heritage professionals to apply for its upcoming peer-learning visit in Rotterdam, The Netherlands, taking place from 17-19 June 2026. Organised in partnership with the City of Rotterdam, this programme offers a unique opportunity to share good practices and acquire skills for addressing environmental, digital, and social challenges related to cultural heritage in local contexts.

The visit follows up on the Hub’s Open Call for Local Good Practices, launched in November 2025, recognising exemplary local solutions in cultural heritage that contribute to the green, digital, and social transformation of our society. Rotterdam was among the 10 selected practices having presented its project “Holy Houses Festival”, celebrating the city’s religious heritages and creativity, which will be the main focus of the visit.

creator: Liv Martin

title: Join Youth.Heritage.Europe.’s 2nd Youth Policy Debate exploring heritage as a driver for empowering local communities
content:
As an affiliated partner of the EU-funded European Heritage Hub, Youth.Heritage.Europe. is organising the second in a series of three Youth Policy Debates, focusing on the theme of “Heritage & Local Communities”. On 16 April 2026 at 14:00 CET, the event will bring together heritage professionals, researchers, and youth representatives to discuss how cultural heritage can act as a catalyst for community empowerment, social innovation, and inclusive local development.

Cultural heritage plays a vital role in supporting regeneration and cohesion, particularly in regions experiencing economic or social challenges. However, its potential often remains underutilised due to limited resources, insufficient community engagement, and governance structures that do not fully include local voices. This debate aims to address these challenges by exploring how participatory approaches and locally rooted initiatives can unlock the transformative power of heritage.
